STATUS ON REMITTITURE
Off Calendar Feb-08-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances (via Zoom videoconference):
Atty. KATHERINE A. RABAGO, Esq. of The Arns Law Firm (Tel:415-495-7800) for Plaintiffs.
Atty. BRADLEY HAMBURGER, Esq. of Gibson, Dunn & Crutcher LLP (Tel: 213-229-7000) for Defendant.

Hearing Re: STATUS ON REMITTITUR.

Matter discussed. The Court holds a discussion regarding the Court of Appeals ruling. Counsel Rabago requests a continuance with leave to file supplemental briefs. Counsel Hamburger requests to send the individual claim to arbitration and stay the remainder of the proceedings. Upon conclusion of discussion, the Court orders as follows: The individual claim is to be sent to arbitration and the remainder of the proceedings shall be stayed.

Counsel Bradley Hamburger is to prepare and submit a proposed order containing the substantive portion of the Court's ruling.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported.(302/RBU)

DEFENDANT POSTMATES, INC.'S Motion For And Order Staying Litigation.

Off calendar as moot given the ruling in the companion motion.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer, Clerk: W. Trupek, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano CSR# 8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com mailto:maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. (302/RBU)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For An Order Compelling Arbitration And Staying Litigation; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Thereof
Off Calendar Mar-22-2022 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances via Zoom Conference
Shaun Mathur, Esq., GIBSON, DUNN & CRUTCHER LLP, 949.451.3800, smathur@gibsondunn.com for Defendant POSTMATES INC.
No ther appearances.

Matter is not contested. No argument is held. Defendant's counsel submits on the Court's tentative ruling.(as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript)

The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT POSTMATES, INC.'S Motion For An Order Compelling Arbitration And Staying Litigation.

Defendant's (POSTMATES, INC.) motion to compel arbitration is denied.

Plaintiff pleads solely a representative PAGA action. (Second Amended Complaint, par. 13.) Plaintiff does not allege any individual claims. The waiver of representative PAGA claims in an arbitration provision-claims that are on behalf of the State of California-is unenforceable and the Court of Appeal has held that the U.S. Supreme Court has yet to abrogate that rule. (See Winns v. Postmates Inc. (2021) 66 Cal.App.5th 803; Iskanian v. CLS Transportation L.A., LLC (2014) 59 Cal.4th 348.) There is therefore nothing to arbitrate.

Defendant's motion for stay is granted. The application for a stay pursuant to CCP 1281.4 is proper even though plaintiff has no individual arbitrable claims. CCP 1281.4 provides:

If a court of competent jurisdiction, whether in this State or not, has ordered arbitration of a controversy which is an issue involved in an action or proceeding pending before a court of this State, the court in which such action or proceeding is pending shall upon motion of a party to such action or proceeding, stay the action or proceeding until an arbitration is had in accordance with the order to arbitrate or until such earlier time as the court specifies.

Here, the employee/independent contractor misclassification dispute is an issue in this case and that issue is presently being arbitrated in several different venues. (See, e.g., Manthripragada Decl., Ex. J.) Given that overlap, a stay is warranted.

Effective Monday, January 3, 2022, for the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number.

Order is signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer, Clerk: W. Trupek, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano CSR# 8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com mailto:maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. (302/RBU)
